title: Opening Keynote: The New Digital Frontier
speaker: Maya Chen
description: An electrifying opening that charts the course for three days of disruption, creativity, and code.

title: Design Systems at Scale: When Atoms Collide
speaker: Rafael Torres
description: How to architect a design system that scales from 10 to 10,000 components without losing your mind.

title: WASM & Rust: The Browser's New Superpower
speaker: Kenji Nakamura
description: Compiling Rust to WebAssembly and unlocking near-native performance in the browser.

title: Diffusion Models: Art at Machine Scale
speaker: Zara Ahmed
description: How diffusion models are fundamentally changing the tools and language of visual creation.

title: Motion Design for the Attention Economy
speaker: Lena Kovacs
description: Crafting animations that hold attention without triggering sensory overload.

title: Edge Runtime: Computing at the Speed of Light
speaker: Sam Okafor
description: Building globally distributed applications with sub-10ms latency using edge compute.

title: Neural Interfaces: When Code Meets Thought
speaker: Dr. Priya Singh
description: A fascinating exploration of brain-computer interfaces and their implications for the future of design.

title: The Brutalist Web Revival
speaker: Finn O'Brien
description: Why the raw, chaotic aesthetic of brutalism is conquering the avant-garde web.

title: WebGPU: Rendering the Impossible
speaker: Yuki Tanaka
description: An in-depth technical journey through the new WebGPU API and its transformative creative possibilities.

title: Ethical AI: Designing Responsible Systems
speaker: Amara Johnson
description: Frameworks for building AI systems that are fair, transparent, and accountable to the humans they serve.

title: Color in the Dark: Accessibility Meets Aesthetics
speaker: Nico Ferreira
description: Building stunning dark interfaces that pass WCAG guidelines without sacrificing visual beauty.

title: The Architecture of Micro-Frontends
speaker: Eva Müller
description: Decomposing monolithic frontends into scalable, team-independent micro-frontends.

title: Generative Music & Code: When Algorithms Compose
speaker: Leo Park
description: Live coding session: generating synchronized music and visuals simultaneously with JavaScript.

title: LLMs for Product Engineers: The Practical Guide
speaker: Cassandra Wu
description: Moving beyond the hype: practical patterns for integrating large language models into real products.

title: Typography as a Living System
speaker: Marco Ricci
description: Variable fonts, optical sizing, and the future of type on the web platform.

title: Real-Time Collaboration: The Hard Parts
speaker: Nadia Petrova
description: CRDTs, operational transforms, and conflict resolution in real-time collaborative applications.

title: Design Engineering: The Missing Link
speaker: Oliver Hayes
description: How design engineers bridge the gap between vision and execution in modern product teams.

title: Spatial Computing for Web Developers
speaker: Aisha Malik
description: Building for visionOS and the next generation of spatial web experiences.

title: Multimodal AI: Seeing, Hearing, Creating
speaker: Jake Morrison
description: How multimodal models are collapsing the barrier between language, vision, and sound.

title: The Creator Economy's Second Wave
speaker: Sofia Delgado
description: Owning your audience, your tools, and your revenue stream in the next creator paradigm.

title: Signals & Fine-Grained Reactivity
speaker: Ryan Carrington
description: Why signals are displacing virtual DOM and how they change how we think about state.

title: AI as Creative Collaborator
speaker: Luna Vasquez
description: An artist's personal journey exploring AI as partner, not just tool, in the creative process.

title: Crafting Immersive Web Experiences
speaker: Ben Clarke
description: Behind the scenes of award-winning digital experiences and the technology stack that powers them.

title: The Future is Open Source
speaker: Maria Santos
description: Building sustainable open source projects and communities in a world driven by commercial incentives.

title: Closing Keynote: Build Like It Matters
speaker: FNSH
description: A rallying call to the builders, makers, and dreamers in the room to create the future they want to live in.
